statement: For every f in V there are finite core functions f_N with f_N -> f in the augmented graph norm and R_aug,N f_N -> R_aug f in the transported graph-dual trace topology.

coreDensityInputStatus.label: core density input
coreDensityInputStatus.reason: The smooth/Galerkin core is dense in the completed augmented graph space V.

traceConvergenceInputStatus.label: augmented trace convergence input
traceConvergenceInputStatus.reason: R_aug,N converges strongly to R_aug in graph-dual trace topology.

moscoLimsupStatus.label: Mosco limsup recovery
moscoLimsupStatus.reason: Choose smooth core approximants by density, then choose finite sections large enough that the graph norm and trace rows converge.

proof: Approximate f in V by a smooth lifted core element g using core density.
Approximate g by finite Galerkin/core sections f_N in graph norm.
Use strong graph-dual convergence of R_aug,N to pass trace coordinates.
Diagonalize the two approximations to obtain f_N -> f and R_aug,N f_N -> R_aug f.
